lede: A look at April 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jenna Jameson and Charles Baudelaire, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tbilisi
events.desc: In Tbilisi, Georgia, up to 60,000 people protest against the government of Mikheil Saakashvili.

events.title: Georgia (country)
events.desc: Georgia declares independence from the Soviet Union.

events.title: Iraq
events.desc: The Iraqi regime of Saddam Hussein kills philosopher Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr and his sister Bint al-Huda after three days of torture.

events.title: Boeing 737
events.desc: The first Boeing 737 (a 100 series) makes its maiden flight.

events.title: Suez Canal
events.desc: The Suez Canal in Egypt is cleared and opens to shipping following the Suez Crisis.

events.title: Journey of Reconciliation
events.desc: The Journey of Reconciliation, the first interracial Freedom Ride begins through the upper South in violation of Jim Crow laws. The riders wanted enforcement of the United States Supreme Court's 1946 Irene Morgan decision that banned racial segregation in interstate travel.

events.title: Tornado outbreak of April 9–11, 1947
events.desc: The Glazier–Higgins–Woodward tornadoes kill 181 and injure 970 in Texas, Oklahoma, and Kansas.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Weserübung: Germany invades Denmark and Norway.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Arras: The battle begins with Canadian Corps executing a massive assault on Vimy Ridge.

events.title: Old Swiss Confederacy
events.desc: Despite being outnumbered 16:1, forces of the Old Swiss Confederacy are victorious over the Archduchy of Austria in the Battle of Näfels.

births.name: Valerie Solanas
births.line: American radical feminist author, attempted murderer

births.name: Leopold II of Belgium
births.line: King of the Belgians from 1865 to 1909

births.name: Léon Blum
births.line: French lawyer and politician, Prime Minister of France

births.name: Chris Watson
births.line: Chilean-Australian journalist and politician, 3rd Prime Minister of Australia

deaths.text: Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, Consort of Elizabeth II from 1952 to 2021
Ibrahim Baré Maïnassara, Nigerien general and politician, President of Niger
Muhammad Baqir al-Sadr, Iraqi cleric and philosopher
Frank Lloyd Wright, American architect, designed the Price Tower and Fallingwater
Francis Bacon, English jurist and politician, Attorney General for England and Wales

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Thomas of Tolentino
